task: Health check: run smoke tests, verify the site loads, check /health endpoint returns 200. If anything fails, describe what's wrong.

assignments.devops: Run the full health check suite: 1) Execute `python3 smoke_test.py` from ~/indiestack/ and report results (pass/fail counts, any failing endpoints). 2) Verify the production site loads by curling https://indiestack.ai/ and https://indiestack.fly.dev/ — check for 200 status codes and that HTML content is returned. 3) Check the /health endpoint specifically: `curl -sL -w '%{http_code}' https://indiestack.fly.dev/health`. If anything fails, describe exactly what's wrong including status codes and error messages.

decomposition.reasoning: This is a straightforward operational health check — purely a DevOps concern. Smoke tests, HTTP status checks, and /health endpoint verification are all infrastructure/deployment tasks. No frontend, backend code changes, content, or MCP work needed. Single department, single execution group.

strategy_review.reasoning: Routine operational hygiene. Site uptime is table stakes — if it's down, nothing else matters. The task is read-only (smoke tests + curl), zero risk of breaking anything, and should complete quickly and cheaply. DevOps is the correct and only department needed. No overengineering here.

approved_tasks.devops: Run the full health check suite: 1) Execute `python3 smoke_test.py` from ~/indiestack/ and report results (pass/fail counts, any failing endpoints). 2) Verify the production site loads by curling https://indiestack.ai/ and https://indiestack.fly.dev/ — check for 200 status codes and that HTML content is returned. 3) Check the /health endpoint specifically: `curl -sL -w '%{http_code}' https://indiestack.fly.dev/health`. If anything fails, describe exactly what's wrong including status codes and error messages.
